response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist engineer with genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and distributed systems exposure from Sociomantic, but the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust for performance-critical routing data pipelines. His C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — neither constitutes commercial systems-level C++ development. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and there is no Rust experience at all. Without the foundational language fit, the geospatial background alone is insufficient to bridge the gap for a mid-senior role building production routing tile pipelines.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development via GISCollective and the Green Map GIS application, including event-driven geo-data architecture
Distributed systems experience at scale — micro-services handling millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic with low-latency, async I/O patterns
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev) and 15+ years of diverse engineering across embedded, web, and mobile
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience; the only C++ work dates to 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis — far below production systems-level proficiency
No routing, navigation guidance, or tiling pipeline domain knowledge; geospatial work is hobbyist/NGO-focused rather than navigation-data scale
Primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang — a fundamentally different stack from the Linux systems programming required by this role
